templates.title: Templates
templates.name: Name
templates.variables_syntax_desc: Use {{.variable_name}} in the subject, text body or HTML body to insert a dynamic variable.
templates.variables_data_desc: Values are provided via the template_data field when sending the mail.
templates.expected_variables_desc: Declare the variables used in the template (documentation for API users).
templates.link_no_link: No links found in the template
templates.link_rate_limited: Check limited to once per minute

tooltip.name: Display name to identify the template in the list
tooltip.slug: URL-safe unique identifier. Leave empty for auto-generation from name
tooltip.text_body: Plain text version of the email, used by mail clients that don't support HTML
tooltip.html_body: HTML version of the email. Supports Go variables: {{.variable}}

tooltip.name: Name to easily identify this SMTP configuration
tooltip.port: SMTP port (587 for STARTTLS, 465 for SSL, 25 unencrypted)
tooltip.auth_method: Authentication method for the SMTP server
tooltip.tls_policy: TLS encryption level required for the connection
tooltip.password: SMTP password (encrypted at rest with AES-GCM)
tooltip.from_email: Email address used as default sender
tooltip.from_name: Name displayed as sender in mail clients
tooltip.charset: Character set for email content encoding

tenants.title: Tenants
tenants.name: Name
tenants.info_line: Rate limit: {{rate}} req/s | Burst: {{burst}} | Max recip.: {{max}} | Priority: {{priority}}
tenants.confirm_regenerate_key: Regenerate API key? The old key will be immediately revoked and will no longer work.

tooltip.name: Name of the organization or project using the API
tooltip.slug: URL-safe unique tenant identifier. Auto-generated if empty
tooltip.rate_limit: Maximum number of mails sent per second
tooltip.rate_burst: Number of mails that can be sent at once (must be >= rate limit)
tooltip.max_recipients: Maximum number of recipients per send request
tooltip.default_priority: Priority applied to mails if not specified in the request
tooltip.spam_threshold: Score above which the spam action is triggered (0-10). Leave empty to disable
tooltip.spam_action: Action to take when spam score exceeds threshold: warn or block sending
tooltip.language: Language used for error messages and tenant notifications
tooltip.store_body: Keeps a compressed (zstd) copy of the HTML/text body after sending. Disabled by default to save disk space

branding.title: Branding
branding.timezone_hint: Used to interpret dates without explicit timezone (e.g.: scheduled_at).
branding.logo_label: Logo image (PNG, JPEG or SVG, max 512 KB)
branding.language_hint: Changes the language of the entire application (UI and API error messages).

help.title: API Usage Guide
help.subtitle: Quick reference for sending mails via the MailHive API.
help.section1_desc: All requests require a JWT token. Obtain one by exchanging your API key:
help.section1_note: The returned token must be included in the Authorization: Bearer <token> header of every request.
help.section2_desc: Send a mail by specifying recipients, subject and message body. The sender is automatically taken from your default SMTP configuration.
help.section2_note: The mail is queued (status pending) and sent asynchronously. The response returns a 202 Accepted code.
help.section4_default: Default: the sender is the one configured in your SMTP config (from_email / from_name).
help.section4_override: One-time override: pass the from field in the request to use a different sender for this mail only.
help.section5_desc: Use a pre-registered template by passing its ID and replacement variables:
help.section5_note: Subject and body are automatically rendered from the template. You can override them by passing them explicitly.
help.section6_desc: Attachments are sent as base64 in the attachments field:
help.section7_desc: With individuel: true, a separate mail is created for each TO recipient. Each recipient can receive specific template variables via template_data at the address level.
help.section7_note: The response returns {"total": 2, "mail_ids": [...]} instead of a single mail. Recipient template_data is merged with shared data (recipient takes priority).
help.section8_desc: Mails are processed through 3 weighted queues. Priority is resolved in this order:
help.section9_retry_desc: On temporary SMTP failure (timeout, connection refused), the mail is retried up to 5 times with exponential backoff (15s, 60s, 240s...). The mail only goes to failed on the last attempt.
help.section9_cb_desc: After 5 consecutive failures on the same SMTP server, a circuit breaker opens and temporarily blocks sends (30s cooldown) to avoid overloading an unavailable server. Mails are automatically retried after cooldown.
help.section9_perm_desc: Configuration errors (template not found, missing SMTP config, authentication error) immediately set the mail to failed without retry.
help.section10_desc: Each SMTP configuration defines the charset and transfer encoding used when sending. These options are configured on the SMTP Configs page.
help.section12_desc: Check the status of a sent mail with its ID:

param.subject: Mail subject (ignored if template provided)
param.smtp_config_id: Specific SMTP config ID (visible on SMTP Configs page)
param.priority: Priority: critical, default or low (default: tenant config)
param.individuel: Create 1 mail per TO recipient (default: false)
param.metadata: Free metadata attached to the mail

ai.title: Generate content with AI
ai.prompt_label: Describe the email you want to create
ai.prompt_placeholder: E.g.: A welcome email for a new user with an account activation button...
